examples: By studying photographs, true artists, he claimed, would be relieved of menial tasks and become free to devote themselves to the more important spiritual aspects of their work.
For instance, say you have a menial job.
In lucky Luxembourg hardly any graduates end up in menial jobs.
The Industrial Age was largely about making those jobs as menial and unskilled as possible.
As a kid, he did menial jobs around the newspaper office, like sweeping the floor.
